paper_title: Sedimentological and palaeohydrological characterization of Late Pleistocene and Holocene tufa mound palaeolakes using trenching methods in the Spanish Pyrenees

methods.description: This method involves excavating trenches to investigate sediments deposited in the inner depression of tufa mounds. The trenches provide artificial exposures to directly characterize the deposits, map stratigraphy and deformation structures, and describe sedimentary facies.

steps.description: Four 46 to 74 m long and 1.5 to 3.0 m deep backhoe trenches with vertical walls and radial orientation were excavated.
A reference orthogonal grid with a spacing of 1 m on the right wall of the trench was used for accurate logging of sedimentological and deformation structures.
steps.searchable_summary: set up a reference grid for logging

steps.description: The stratigraphy and deformation structures exposed in one of the walls of each trench were mapped at 1:50 scale.
Sedimentary facies were described visually and by microscopic observations on smear slides.
steps.searchable_summary: describe sedimentary facies visually and microscopically

methods.description: This method involves geochemical analyses of samples from trench T3 to determine the contents of various elements and to perform statistical analysis to infer environmental processes.

steps.description: Sampling was performed at 10 to 20 cm intervals along four detailed logs evenly distributed within the logged trench wall.
A total of 31 samples was analysed for Total Carbon (TC), Total Organic Carbon (TOC), Total Inorganic Carbon (TIC), Total Nitrogen (TN) and Total Sulphur (TS).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze samples for TC, TOC, TIC, TN, and TS

steps.description: The TC, TOC and TS contents were determined with a LECO SC 144 DR elemental analyser.
steps.searchable_summary: determine TC, TOC, and TS using LECO analyser

steps.description: The TIC was obtained by subtracting TOC from TC.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate TIC by subtracting TOC from TC

steps.description: The TN content was determined using a VARIO MAX CN element analyser.
steps.searchable_summary: determine TN using VARIO MAX CN analyser

steps.description: Samples were digested with HNO3 and HCl in a microwave oven and analysed by optical emission spectrometry using ICP-OES.
steps.searchable_summary: digest samples and analyze using ICP-OES

steps.description: A statistical analysis of the geochemical data set (Principal Component Analyses) was carried out using the SPSS 20.0 software.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCA on geochemical data using SPSS

methods.description: This method involves collecting samples for radiocarbon and optically stimulated luminescence (OSL) dating to determine the age of sedimentary sequences and reconstruct the chronological evolution of the artesian spring system.

steps.description: Samples for anisotropy of magnetic susceptibility radiocarbon and OSL dating were collected in trenches T1, T3 and T4.
steps.searchable_summary: collect samples for radiocarbon and OSL dating

steps.description: Radiocarbon ages were calibrated using the software package OxCal 4.2 and the data set IntCal 13.
An age-depth model for the composite log of trench T3 was obtained by linear interpolation using the Analyseries software.
OSL ages on aeolian quartz grains from two samples collected in trench T1 were calculated in the Luminescence Dating Research Laboratory.
